2606.30371.title: MaDI-Bench: An End-to-End Data Integration Benchmark
2606.30371.paper_summary: Data integration combines heterogeneous data sets into a single, coherent representation. Data integration involves a sequence of interdependent tasks including schema matching, value normalization, entity blocking, entity matching, and data fusion. Existing benchmarks either evaluate these steps in isolation or cover only incomplete versions of the data integration pipeline, omitting specific steps. The lack of public end-to-end data integration benchmarks hinders research on data integration methods that address the integration process as a whole. This paper fills this gap by introducing the Mannheim Data Integration Benchmark (MaDI-Bench), the first benchmark for the end-to-end integration of relational tables covering all steps of the integration process. MaDI-Bench contributes (i) a set of base end-to-end data integration tasks spanning several application domains, each requiring the full schema matching, value normalization, entity matching, and conflict resolution pipeline; and (ii) a generic method for deriving task variants that mitigates rapid benchmark saturation as data integration systems advance. We validate the benchmark using human-engineered pipelines, a best-of-breed pipeline, and an LLM-based pipeline. The validation demonstrates the utility of the benchmark for measuring the step-wise as well as the end-to-end performance of data integration pipelines. All benchmark artifacts are available for public download.
2606.30371.paper_summary_zh: 資料整合將異質數據集結合成單一且一致的表示。資料整合涉及一系列相互依賴的任務，包括模式匹配、值標準化、實體阻擋、實體匹配和數據融合。現有的基準要麼孤立地評估這些步驟，要麼僅涵蓋不完整的資料整合流程，省略了特定步驟。缺乏公共的端到端資料整合基準妨礙了針對整體整合過程的資料整合方法的研究。本文通過介紹曼海姆資料整合基準（MaDI-Bench）來填補這一空白，這是首個涵蓋整個整合過程所有步驟的關聯表的端到端整合基準。MaDI-Bench 貢獻了 (i) 一組涵蓋多個應用領域的基本端到端資料整合任務，每個任務都需要完整的模式匹配、值標準化、實體匹配和衝突解決流程；以及 (ii) 一種通用方法來衍生任務變體，以減輕隨著資料整合系統進步而快速飽和的基準問題。我們使用人為設計的流程、一個最佳實踐流程以及基於 LLM 的流程來驗證該基準。驗證顯示了該基準在測量資料整合流程的逐步性能以及端到端性能方面的實用性。所有基準文檔均可供公眾下載。
2606.30371.author: Aaron Steiner et.al.
2606.30371.authors: Aaron Steiner, Ralph Peeters, Christian Bizer
